reviews.review_text: We booked the Le grand chalet and all I can say is that it was amazing. The cleanliness, service and facilities were great. Will definitely be back!
We have an ensuite suite. Fantastic pad for the van and the ensuites are very comfortable and clean, very quiet the rest of the facilities are new. Nice place

We stayed in Cape Le Grande cabin no. 17 for 3 nights in the second week of May. The cabin was clean, spacious, modern and very well maintained.
It had everything that one would need for a comfortable and pleasant stay.
I would definitely recommend the place. This was one of the very good caravan park cabins that we have stayed in Australia.

Stayed in the newer section, large sites, with good drainage from rain , wide road and good access for larger vans, sites have new synthetic turf.
Very helpful staff at book in counter
Great spot to camp Clean facilities
Fantastic ensuite site with ample room to park the camper. Amenities were clean and well taken care of.

We booked this campsite for a long weekend as all the other campsites were sold out already. It wasn’t a very pleasant stay, the bathrooms are in desperate need of an upgrade. They were also in really bad need of cleaning, every time I used the bathroom over the 4 days I was there it was extremely unpleasant. The campsite also flooded with the rain, and due to the site not being fully tarmac there were a huge number of potholes and it was so mucky and wet. Disappointing considering it was the Easter weekend.

We had a lovely stay here. The park is lovely and clean and well maintained.  We had a great spot with our van. Big spots so not on top of each other. Tried to use the showers but was hit and miss. Hot water was on and off so just used my own shower in the van. Highly recommend this place.
We enjoyed an awesome stay  Great location to explore from and friendly staff.
Great place friendly staff big sites value for the sites
Excellent service! Very helpful staff.

Located a few klms out of town. Very nice quiet treed caravan park. Amenities are cleaned daily and well set out. Washing machines/dryers are tap and go. This is the fifth time we have stayed here (we’re from Melbourne) and we are always happy with our stay.
A terrific quiet park. Quaint little garden beds, a herb garden and friendly 👌 staff. Pet friendly.

Great location. Big sites and amenities and park are well maintained. Big shout out to assistant managers Mon and Gary for being so welcoming and attentive. They made our stay so enjoyable we didn’t want to leave.

Extremely unhappy with my stay. The girl at reception was unnecessarily rude and condescending on a number of occasions - when I asked where the laundry room was I was told “where the laundry symbol is on the map”. The first night we were placed on a site in the middle of a group of bikers who were there before us, who could easily have been placed on adjoining sites, and not been shouting across at each other across us. The grassy site (L7) where we were placed the second night was a dirt patch on the side of the road beside a skip and not acceptable at all for the price paid. There were also no taps nearby. The kitchen is tiny and the showers were unpleasant.

This place was well located (kind of) and we were able to book last minute (very good) but it wasn’t very nice and the kitchen was disgusting. Not clean at all (there were worms) and it was very small for all the people staying there.

The bathroom was big but the showers were clogged and there were also worms in there.
But as soon as we asked someone to fix it, they did.

Other than that, good place.
There’s phone reception there.
Very nice clean good security

Such a lovely park, plenty of room, love the artificial grass on the caravan sites a brilliant idea. Nice clean amenities, could do with a few more clothes dryers in the laundry’s. The staff are super kind and friendly, we are thoroughly enjoying our stay.
DREADFUL CABIN NO HOT BROKEN TV AND TOILETS TOO FAR AWAY FROM CABIN, I WAS SO UPSET FROM BEING ROBBED OF WORKING TV. DON'T GO THERE THE PLACE IS PACKED OUT BEWARE

Great park older park at the front newer sites at the back that are pretty large we had an ensuite site that had a large pad of Astro turf
Will be back for sure
Staff are super friendly and helpful
A very nice e park. We were in the new section at the rear of the park on an onsuot site. Synthetic grass pad, very clean and plenty of room. Ensuit was very good.

We had a pretty good stay overall, our site was comfortable and spacious.

Down points:
1- definitely too expensive
2: one kitchen for the amount of people is not enough, you want to cook and need to wait up to 1-2 hours because there are always sooooo many people.
3: a staff member was a bit arrogant to us (although the other were nice) so this definitely let the score from 4 down to 2
Das war der lauteste Campingplatz auf dem ich je gewesen bin. Direkt wirklich direkt neben der Hauptstraße. Ich habe kaum Schlaf bekommen und bereue hergekommen zu sein.

I strongly do not recommend Pink Lake Caravan Park.

I was charged twice for the same booking. Despite providing clear proof (bank statement and screenshot), they refused to issue a refund.

Their excuse ? They claim that I made two bookings at the same time, which is completely false. Instead of properly checking their system or taking responsibility for a possible mistake, they simply blamed the customer.

On top of that, the two staff members at reception were very rude. They kept interrupting me and spoke to me as if I were an idiot, rather than calmly trying to understand and resolve the issue.

No accountability and extremely disappointing customer service.

I would strongly recommend staying at RAC Esperance Holiday Park instead — much better campground and impeccable customer service.

Catastrophic customer experience! Don't go there...

​We paid on the website, but they charged us a second time. When we went to see them to make a complaint, the front desk staff,especially the lady—were extremely arrogant. We showed them proof that we only had one single reservation. I have rarely seen people so dishonest. The site wasn't even full, yet they refused to issue any refund...

​The site itself is actually ok except dirty toilets however, the "pink lake" isn't pink. Just one more reason not to go to this caravan park.

​To sum up: there are other parks nearby, so prioritize those if they have availability.

Wish you a good journey in Esperance ! 😁
Good spot toilets just shouldn’t be with the shower or just chuck a squeegee in each of them because it gets really wet and puddles everywhere

The "room" we were put in was their "Workers Quarters", it wasn't like the picture online.  When we arrived, I almost burst into tears. They wouldn't refund our money, and they didn't care.  We went to the RAC Holiday Park, where we were treated like human beings, not just for the money.

We stayed here for 3 nights. The cabin they gave us was obviously the worst one they had. It was filthy, part of the lino was stuck together with gaffa tape, the TV on the wall was stuck with gaffa tape. There were holes in the skirting boards & we even saw cockroaches in the cabin. I don't think they have ever renovated that cabin since the park has opened. On the 2nd day my husband went to the office to tell them that we are not happy with the conditions of the cabin. The manager of the park was the person he spoke to. He couldn't give a damn about it & told my husband that we need to speak to the owners about it.  If he had given us the contact number of the owners I would have contacted them direct. We gave him the opportunity to rectify the problem but he didn't want to know about it. That is why I am writing this review hoping that the owners will see it. When you have managers look after the parks you expect them to do a good job & keep the park clean & tidy. They get paid enough money to do that job. This park has nothing of that. The grounds are not looked after either. When you have tourists like us travelling around Australia we are the ones that are going to recommend parks to stay at. I would definitely not recommend this park to no one. Even the location is not that great.
Great little cozy twilight cabin
clean facility shady site and nice position walking distance to AGI bakery and take away restaurant
Friendly Park staff. Greated with a smile in re eption. Evidence of Park upgrade. Good location. Ablution blocks adequate, clean and tidy.
been here 2 times and always clean and nice  with  shady site . take away restaurant and IGA in walking distance

Pleasant service at the front desk upon arrival.
Easy manoeuvrable spot for our long caravan.
Showers are hot and have great pressure.
Washing and drying available $6 per load.

Beautifully close to an IGA, minimal traffic.
9 minutes drive to Esperence town centre.
45 minute drive to Cape le Grand.

Easy, affordable, covers all essentials - power, water, amenities.

Ze reageren totaal niet op mails.
Hebben er 1 nacht gestaan en wilde mijn andere Booking voor over 2 nachten annuleren via mail.
Maar totaal geen respons, krijg wel een mail voor de check-in, maar als ik daar nogmaals op reageer dat ik de dag er voor een mail heb gestuurd voor de annulering, blijft het weer stil. Vreemd.
Stayed for a night on a powered site, access to clean toilets and hot showers. 5 minute drive to town and 2 minute drive to Bread Local. Would stay again 🙂

What we liked:|• Good location|• Supermarket not too far away|• Out-of-hours check-in was possible|• Fridge, kettle, microwave and toaster in the room||What we didn’t like:|• Very small room, much smaller than it looks in the photos|• Strong smell from nearby shared toilets|• Noise from the neighbouring cabin; could hear everything|• Smoker next door made it impossible to use the outdoor space or keep the door open|• No Wi-Fi|• Late check-in instructions required an Australian mobile number|• Poor room layout: very little space around the bed, rubbish bin next to the bed, chairs had to be folded to fit suitcases on the floor|• Table was dirty and had wine stains|• Parking space was very difficult to use due to the layout and nearby rubbish bins; car had to be partly on the road

We had a powered site, good size and another family member a cabin (an older style Cabin 8) |Cabin was pet friendly, we didn't have a pet but yard fenced and sadly could smell it in the cabin but overall very clean and tidy.|Outdated bathroom in the cabin not good for someone that may have mobility issues as a huge step into the shower over a very tiny bath.|Amenity block could use an update.|Camp kitchen was a good size and user friendly. BBQs x 2|Laundry available and pay by card which is a good way cause if youre like me i didn't have change.  $6 a load.

Pink City CP was a great stay
Although some of their facilities were older they were still clean.
The grounds are well kept neat and clean.
The staff could not have been more helpful helping us out when we needed something printed ( 13 pages ) each person we dealt with was pleasant
Pleny of toilet blocks, laundries and camp kitchens
Highly recommended and we will be back
Safe , clean , nice staff, beautiful environment |And friendly campers, matching prices, close to the centre town, quiet
Great location ,good facilities and well maintained. Stayed in the new section which was great. Good value through Gday|Definitely will star here again

가성비 아주 좋음
혼자 썼지만 4명까지 잘 수 있는 캐빈이어서 사람들이랑 같이 가면 매우 좋았을 것 같음

The checkout experience unfortunately soured what had otherwise been a pleasant stay.

On arrival, I was given a tattered card holder for the gate. When I returned the same holder at checkout, the man at reception responded with “what happened here?!” and, after I explained I received it that way, he accusingly scoffed and said nothing more.

There was no thanks for staying or well wishes. Just an insinuation that I was being dishonest. Left a poor final impression. Unnecessary and unprofessional.

A great holiday / caravan park just 3 minutes from the town center and providing easy access to all the local tourist attractions. A wide variety of accommodation from tents sites to en-suite powered caravan sites. We had a powered site in the new area of the park. Very tidy and appreciated the astroturf base. Great facilities and very clean. Would certainly stay again without hesitation.
Great experience, the site was perfectly level and covered in artificial grass. The location was superb. Lots of kids but no noise. Relaxing few days knowing Esperance

Great caravan park, ask for new section as double  slabs use artificial grass and sone have attached bathrooms per site. It would be good of they could do the rest of the park that way, would make it a standout caravan park.
Really accommodating for myself and my nine family members, and a fantastic location everything is right near by! Would 100% stay again
Great location nice and quiet

Great location, just outside the town. Friendly staff. Hot showers. Bathrooms look on the older side but function well. Each plot has their own green tarp which really helps keep the camper clean. Would definitely recommend & would stay again

This is our last night and this has been a lovely stay for us. The site we were on was lovely with big trees providing shade so you can sleep in a bit in the morning, lovely artifical grass on our site, which made it extra nice, dog friendly, so our dogs could also camp with us and everything was neat and clean and very affordable. We all, especially our son enjoyed our stay here, as he was able to ride his bike up and down in a safe environment and will be sad to leave tomorrow. Also nice to see there are booms so nobody can enter without a key card. We will definitely be visiting in the future. Van Dyk family
Stayed in our caravan good sites good atmosphere,  school holidays are busy though.

Sites small and packed in. En suite sites look nice. Ablutions old and pretty dirty. Tiles need replacing and/or a good scrub. Had items stolen from washing line. Good for young families looking to let their kids run wild!

Great place to stay! Clean baths and kitchen. Inside and outside area either plenty of space. Two sinks to wash fishes. Kitchen with oven, microwave and stoves. A tennis table and couple of toys for kids. Not all the showers have a lot of pressure but one of them was perfect.
Staff were very helpful and being holiday season very busy. Facilities were clean considering park was full but very dated. Could do with an upgrade. All in all a great short caravan stay.
This Caravan park has got the basic amenities but doesn’t have a pool.
Great little stay. Clean, friendly staff and allowed dogs. Highly recommend, with great prices.
Zeer verzorgde camping

We had an Ensuite powered site. Very spacious site. Double artificial grass sites. Lovely planting between sites. Looks quite a new area at the back of the caravan park.
Spotlessly clean amenities.
Nice quiet position 5mins out of town by car. We had a great stay. Thank you to all the staff.
Great place

I am writing to express my disappointment with my recent stay at this caravan park. I had booked a cabin with an ensuite for one night, but upon arrival from Perth, I was informed that the only available accommodation was a trailer on wheels, not a cabin. Apparently, refunds are not offered for changes of mind, despite the fact that I had specifically requested a cabin, not a trailer. Furthermore, the trailer was adjoined to the neighboring accommodation, resulting in noise disturbances throughout the night. The trailer was also unclean, with sticky tape holding down the flooring, a dirty bandaid and bloodstains on the bedding. I am extremely dissatisfied with my experience and would advise others to avoid cabin 15 at this park at all costs.
Great caravan park with awesome staff.
Lovely park. Beautiful sites and amenities and 3min drive to the beach. The staff are sweet. The park is well maintained and clean.
Excellent service and great self contained accommodation. Perfect location, near all the best beaches. Very accommodating to families. Highly recommend.

Just spent 4 nights at Pink Lake CP and loved it.
Usually prefer to be off grid, but this place is special.
Beautiful and clean, great big camp sites so you are not on top of each orher.
5 minutes to town site and staff were great to deal with.
Play ground for kids and fantastic camp kitchen and barbi areas.
Couldnt fault the park, congratulations to the staff for keeping the park so spotless.
Steve and Lauren Batten
Great cabin only a couple of yrs old. Everything was perfect staff friendly and helpful other parks looked run down or very costly.
Yep my goto place with the camper trailer. Plenty of room. On sweet shower & toilet room 💯. 2nd time there in 6 weeks. And it quite.

Older cabins look okay from the outside but are terrible on the in small cramped vinyl floor torn and peeling up no photos of inside when you book very disappointing for a gday park when asked at front dest for an alternative  room or reimbursement  both were denied park manager wouldn't even come talk about it just left the poor office staff to deal with it
Nice place good ppl

A true gem of a caravan park, with great staff and facilities to match.
Grounds and amenities are clean and tidy also pet's are welcome too.
It was fine.

Stayed a couple of nights before heading to Lucky Bay. Very clean park. All the facilities you need, and the staff are very friendly, especially the lady at reception, I think her name is Mon. We will be back again one day.

I booked a budget cabin for a quick 2 night stay. Beds very hard, and can feel the springs. Communal showers in the budget cabin area are really bad and need shower heads replaced so just walk a bit further down to the ones at the Laundry block for much better water pressure.
Service was good and staff were lovely.

Thanks to Tim, the Manager, and staff for a great experience at Pink Lake Tourist Park. We stayed on a powered site with our caravan amongst trees in the older section. This suited us perfectly as we have a dog and there was more lawn available in this area. The sites are generous in size with ground covers and facilities are clean and maintained.  Great park, great location. Highly recommended.
It’s a good camping. Nice kitchen! New part of the camp has no trees yet. Old amenities are bit smelly, new ones are good.
The room was great for the price. They were very accomodating
One of the best caravan Park's we had an ensuite just Great.

AVOID THIS PLACE IF YOU CAN.

The management are not friendly at all. I got reprimanded for walking through the boom gates, using the disabled showers, and having a friend stop on our camp site for a beer. I got told off for washing my vehicle ( with water in my tanks from Norseman) yet, the manager was seen washing his van and car from the hose on site..The boom gates are faulty and will close on your car/van and management will blame you
you...the young ladies are great....but the older managers need to move on...this is not the right job for them...
A fantastic park. Very tidy and well organised. The office staff were also very friendly.
Lovely place to stay even with a pet.
Almost very good 👍
Nice place, clean, decent size sites. Leafy and comfortable
Great place to rv or tent ⛺️ camping
Very reasonable price and not far from town no need to spend big bucks on Abnb

Great park! We stayed in the newer section out the back which offered spacious sites with artificial grass and lovely ensuite style amenities. Not much shade but wasn't an issue. The camp kitchen was fabulous and included an oven so I was able to bake some goodies for the road. Would definitely recommend a stay here.

The sites here at the back of the park are wonderful. Huge fake grass pads and plenty of room for big rigs to manoeuvre. Gardens and surroundings are neat and tidy. Highly recommended. Very reasonable rates too.
The twilight cabin that we stayed in is very nice and clean. Just a heads up it’s a joint cabin.
We stayed over at Cape Le Grande chalet, and we enjoyed the stay.
A great van park. Huge sites and close to the great coastal drives.

This is the best park I have stayed in so far. I doubt any will beat it. Dead flat, wide, easy bays for your caravan. Clean, neat, well maintained.
Defo staying here on my way back.
Excellent location.  Quite!  Nice grounds and amenities.   Reasonable cost.

I had a hard time booking through a third party website for Dog Friendly accommodation but EPLTP showed through! Thank goodness I sent through an email because the booking process was so easy. I also really enjoyed the communication. We had to use a shared bathroom/toilet during off-peak season, this was very easy to use and well-kept.
Great place to hang your hat for a few nights. Disappointed with the pay to use laundry machines. Other than that highly recommend

Good amenities and nice staff.
Thanks!

The park has developed new caravan sites at the rear of the park, with further upgrades to occur on other parts of the park. The sites have artificial grass. The amenities are made of a number of individual toilet/showers. The caretakers continually ensure the park is kept clean. Nice under cover camp kitchen. The staff are friendly and helpful.
The park is about 2 kms from Esperance
Will definitely stay here again

Stopped is as vehicle going in for service and the caravan park was very handy.
Place very well maintained with all you need for a stay, either short or something longer.

Such a wonderful stay! The park is clean, well-maintained, and has everything we needed for a relaxing holiday. The staff were welcoming and helpful, and the facilities were perfect. We especially loved the peaceful atmosphere and beautiful surroundings. We’ll definitely be coming back and highly recommend it to anyone looking for a great getaway! The only thing that I think still can be improve is network connection (wifi) because we had poor reception during our stay. Not really big issue tho since we were busy with outdoor activities most of the times.
Friendly staff and the park was tidy and clean

STAY AWAY!!! If I could give this place -5 stars I would.
We do a lot of trips around WA and this is easily THE WORST park we have ever stayed at.
The customer service from Michelle and her husband Mark is by far the most awful experience we have ever had. Rude, unhelpful, unapologetic and unprofessional. Both should choose a different profession if they are not fired beforehand. The way we were treated by these individuals was outright disgusting and should be reported if I’m being honest.
Booked a campsite 8 months in advance in the new part of the park and when we arrived we were pushed to an old campsite at the front of the park with no warning. They had overbooked the same campsite, yet despite this being their fault there was no apology given, nothing done to rectify the situation and no accountability taken on their part. The newer campsites cost extra and Michelle tried to charge us MORE for being moved to an older campsite without asking us first. She is a con artist and her husband is just as bad. Both were aggressive and intimidating during conversations about the issue. Totally inappropriate for park managers. The least someone can do is offer an apology. Facilities were disgusting, unclean and old. Showers sucked and hot water wasn’t even a thing.

We will never be back and suggest you never come here!

Friendly staff ,  not helpful site manager.
My experience: pretty old cabin , cold and gets cold drafts at night , not insulated, old split aircon makes loud noise, gaps near window and light hole, hot water availability- poor , not so clean carpet and dirty couch pillows. Kitchen was poorly structured, with poor exhaust.
Kids play area needs clean , fence for safety as it’s close to car park
And road .

-1 star the park has been reported to the police commissioner of wa and the anti discrimination commission for abuse threats and intimidation of woman with a service assistance dog … the manager aggressively approached me shaking I feared for my safety said  I’m sick of people with disabilities and being intitied and privileged who have service assistance dogs yes Tim said  manager of the park  … ( for your information Tim they are our life saviors … I hope your charged with a crime under the crime bill… oh wait there is nothing in the crimes act and it’s ok to abuse people with disabilities who have services therapy dogs incase people with disabilities are ignored for making serious complaints and also threatened further for making complaints let alone educate people about how serious they have been victimized   … i posting this for other people who need to be informed about this situation at this caravan park … I wear a full body camera for my safety and protection and I’m happy to hand it over to the commissioner of police Wa for the crime let alone political parties in support  (Thank you for contacting the Office of the Hon. Reece Whitby MLA, Minister for Police; Road Safety; Tourism; Great Southern.) — let alone lazy politicians who think it’s ok to let this happen to myself and other people who have service assistance therapy dogs or other therapy animals…. We are not intitied they save our lives …

Fabulous caravan park. The staff were friendly and extremely helpful throughout our stay.  The sites are spacious with synthetic grass and pebble borders. Each site is demarcated with a row of bushes so the space feels like your own.

The facilities were spotless and the park was a wonderful quiet spot only 2.5km to the esplanade in Esperance.

Perfectly situated for access to Pink Lake, Great Ocean Drive and Cape Le Grand National Park.

Highly recommended

Di at the front desk was fantastic
The new section at the back of the park is fantastic
Excellent all round. Staff, peaceful location, laundry. We had a cabin. Very comfortable. Thanks!
Ensuite sites are excellent . Staff very helpful
Fantastic place to stay friendly staff comfy bed clean good self contained cabins stayed for a week to explore the beautiful esperance and region was a great home base
Nice and quiet, great caravan sites. Amusing clean amenities with everything in the unisex facilities ( toilet, basin and shower) Helpful staff upon booking in!
We had a cabin and it was large and fairly well appointed. It had a card operated laundry, which saved looking for coins !! 🙂🙂
We stayed for 3 nights and enjoyed our time there. Staff were very helpful, amenities were great. Location was awesome every thing we needed was a short walk from the park.
Very pleased with my time there. Bit pricey, but the Tuesday deal is handy for those who can take advantage of it, which I did.

Could not fault our stay.
Michelle & Tomo were very welcoming.
Michelle opened back up for me on Sunday night so I could get a couple icecreams..
Amazing not many places do that.
Lovely park. Clean amenities.

Fantastic caravan park. Very warm reception by Michelle & Tomo. We were in the new section which is brilliant. Very generous site with synthetic turf on the ground. Very clean amenities. The older part of the park appears clean and tidy as well. The only downside is the people travelling with dogs, who let them constantly bark, but this is not the parks fault. Just inconsiderate travellers.

Last minute booking. Only affordable place available for x1 night for a family of x4 + x1 sml dog.

PROS: Excepts pets, cozy, well stocked with cutlery, cooking utensils.

CONS: On the website said Linen provided,  but when checking in there was no linen on the bunk beds for the kids... only x1 blanket n x2 pillows in the cupboard. Only x2 towels provided.  Had to ask for linen/blankets/towels for the kids. No air-conditioning,  only sml fan heater (thank god), no fans, no hot water in cabin. Cabin door doesnt close properly unless locked.

Toilet/showers in shared area outside cabins. Showers in ladies bathroom was very disappointing.... no hot water, have to run around in the shower to get wet. Facilities were clean, but old.
Ok for x1 night
Nice park

Fantastic staff. Mark and Michelle new to the park but doing an awesome job
Sites are awesome
Amenities clean

This is a great park to stay at, friendly staff, well maintained, lots of amenities a great camp kitchen with a fab herb garden you can use.  Sites are quite spacious and marked out clearly.  Recommend it without any hesitation

We had a great stay for two nights. The sites are roomy and we had a green mat too. It’s a peaceful park with no noise at night. Staff were very friendly and helpful. Even had its own herb garden which I assume guests could use. Showers were awesome and amenities clean. Would recommend this park for travellers.

I have edited my previous review since returning recently. The staff here are still very pleasant and helpful and many of my earlier criticisms have been addressed. Bathrooms are clean and tidy and the laundry is modern.

My previous review

It was unseasonably busy so we were lucky to get in however it was a bit disappointing, the sites are pretty tight and the place is somewhat tired and lacks maintenance. The facilities are jaded and although they are cleaned daily they still appear a bit grubby. The place needs an upgrade. Locks not working, taps leaking, dirty shower curtains , dirty grout, the mop in the showers were rotten and half the head was gone. Many of these things could be solved in expensively. Having said all of that the gardens were very nicely kept.

Perfect spot. Far enough away from main roads close enough to beach and town. Everything is clean prices are easily cheaper than anywhere else in esperance. Best of all... Its faced against the wind so even on a cold day it's warmest place in town 😜😊😊😊
Caravan bays were good with artificial grass as pad. Very quiet with lovely trees. Ablution blocks could do with a facelift but were generally clean. Laundry machines were clean and looked fairly new
neatly kept room, services and utilities were accessible. quiet area. although gates locks at 9, so cant stay out late

2 nights, one was ensuite on New side of Park which was very cleaned and tidy. One night was cabin stay which was OK as its bit older and need some maintainence but no issues. Staff was friendly but some miscommunication from their end, i guess that was bcos of easter pressure as they were fully booked.
Nice place to stay.
I ENJOYED.

Decent facilities epically in the new side of the site. The older sites are oddly laid out.

Single easiest upgrade would be to not use single ply for all the bathrooms..

It’s Easter & I understand it’s busy, with loads of families.
There are 2 sections to the park, which I did not know on booking, the noisy, packed in like sardines, dated, sad, dirty amenities, patchy grassy looking spot near your site & a particularly patchy dirty holey bit of green tarp? Earwigs making a nest under the tarp thing.
Dogs off leash & wandering the park, a dog right next to us that barked & growled at anyone that came near their van & jumped at my husband mere centimetres from his face & tried to get at me from under the van.
My husband approached them in a few occasions about their dog.

People waking up at 6am & made damn sure the rest of the people in the area around them woke up at that time too.

What really irked me was the amenities (block 3) & the condition people left them in… one morning there was water all over the floor, toilet paper all over the floor, hair hanging off the benches, mud everywhere & a group of women & children totally oblivious to the mess.
I was a cleaner, in a previous life, & my heart went out to the amazing cleaning staff that had to deal with that.
Some people take advantage of these facilities with free abandon.. and it’s wrong.

The “top” section of the park was like walking into an entirely different caravan park, quiet, huge sites (have no idea if they are the same cost as the “sardines” area we were booked in). Clean amenities, sometimes a bit wet & yuk, a huge green grassy mat by your front door & very well maintained with beautiful gardens…

The laundry washer & dryers are $6 each per load!?!?
The highest I have ever seen on my travels!

Moral to my lengthy story?
Book in to the new section… I booked months in advance & was left to the powers that be to book me “somewhere”.

Not at all happy.
Great little park shame about the service in front office. If you don't like dealing with the public, then maybe, thus, isn't the job for you.
Nice clean park great stay

Wir waren für eine Nacht über die Ostertage mit einem Campervan auf diesem Campinglplatz. Im Vergleich zu anderen Campingplätzen in WA war die Sauberkeit hier leider nicht gut. Duschen, WCs und Badräume befinden sich auf einem alten Stand. Geruch und Sauberkeit ließen hier bei Männern und Frauen zu wünschen übrig.

Die camp-kitchen ist okay, aber nichts besonderes. Standort ist in Ordnung und von der ocean-Route gut zu erreichen.

Das WLAN ist hervorragend.

We are exploring the SW of WA and needed a site in Esperance for a while, this became difficult due availability in town. Got into the Pink Lake cvan park and very happy with it😊. Huge very tidy sites, double artificial turf (which was excellent with 60mm of rain over the last few days), and the mangers here couldn't be better.
We recommend it highly, but make sure you ask for the new sites up the back, the rest of the park is a bit old.
For what we paid, we expected a bit more than what we got. But I guess it stopped us getting wet

Great park catering for all age groups. Extremely clean amenities and close to shopping and beaches.
Would be happy to return.
A great place to spend a few days.
Great caravan park with nice new area out the back which you should ask for when booking.

This is such a pretty caravan park. Location is great and staff are exceptional. We had a family emergency and had to leave early, staff were compassionate and placed our missed stay on a client account to use on our next trip to Esperance. Something I can say the next place we were to stay at weren’t willing to do. So thanks again Pink Lake Caravan Park we really appreciate it!! Daz n Ange

Got stranded in Esperance due to our car breaking down. The staff was so nice about our situation and helped in every way they could! Amenities are nice and the caravan park overall is well maintained. Thanks again for your hospitality!

On arrival the staff were so pleasant to deal with, despite the fact that their was a very intoxicated man in front if us at the front counter ( we could see his goon bag in his backpack) they handled the situation very kindly, dignifying him and all those waiting in line. Well done!!
The park was immaculate, gardeners still working at 4.30. The camp kitchen was well equipped, great gas stove and a few pots n pans if you needed a few things extra or forgot to bring pots. Washing up facilities and clean tea towels for use.  Loved the addition of the herb garden, we used chilli's and coriander.  The bathroom facilities were clean, spacious, included was, a shower and toilet in the 1 room.  Perfect!! Super stoked!! Wish we had stayed longer....... thank you !!
Nice caravan park
Very clean park, with great facilities. Friendly staff and reasonable rates.

We stayed in one of the older, smaller cabins, which was comfortable, but next time, we’ll be sure to book earlier to secure one of the newer cabins—they look beautiful! Overall, we had a lovely stay and enjoyed our time.

We stayed for one night at Esperance Pink Lake Tourist Park. It’s clear that the campground is split into two sections, with the second part being brand new.

We were on a powered site in the newer area, which had a stabilized surface (not sand), and it was really pleasant. However, there weren’t many trees, and the sites were a bit on the small side.

A big positive was the new facilities—the bathrooms are individual, private rooms, which was a great touch. The camp kitchen was well-equipped, though it was quite small and got crowded with people. There’s also a BBQ available for use.

Another plus was the flexible check-in time, which made it easy for us. Overall, it was a nice stop, though it could be a bit more spacious.

Be Aware When Booking a Cabin !!!

The cabin we were assigned was so small.
There was only enough room for the double bed and a small gap on either side and end. Could barely move around in here.

In the same demountable was another cabin space on the other side.
The bathrooms met in the middle.
All throughout the night we could hear the people in the other side snoring and getting up for a poo and multiple wees.
Could not sleep at all.
Each time they opened the sliding door it rattles our side.
This kind of accommodation should not be provided these days.
If this was advertised as the cabin we were getting upon booking we 100% would have avoided as it was not at all pleasent.

This is like two separate parks.  The front is the original or older with sandy and small sites and some permanent residents.  The back of the park is new with synthetic grass for both your van and awning and plenty of room to park on the gravel in front of and around your site.  Ablutions are en-suite style with toilet, shower and basin and are kept very clean.  There are some sites in this are available with private ensuites   There is a small IGA and bottle shop close to the park but they are not as well stocked as Woolies and another IGA in town (which BTW close on Sundays!).  We used Abalee Kennels to look after our two small dogs for the day to visit the beaches in the National Park ($45 total for the day)
Would recommend this park so long as you book in the new section
Great facilities, good size sites. We used the en suite and they were definitely worth the extra charge. Very clean everywhere and good laundry room with an iron if needed.
Nice park with easy access sites, some shady some not. Amenities clean and functional. Very fast check-in process.

Accommodating staff, we asked for a transfer to another spot for our caravan 🚐  coz in which we  where at first got no shade and so open, we wanted to atleast protect our caravan from the strong winds and to set up our annex' and the staff where so kind enough to accommodate us....thank u'

Best camp sight in my opinion! On the pricey side but worth the money! Hot very good pressure showers, and clean toilets. Good kitchen, but no cutlery so bring your own! Quiet at night, very secure with code to get in, local bread down the road is brilliant for baked goods and coffee! Would definitely recommend and stay again
Excellent caravane park, les douches/toilettes privées sont très propres et très modernes. Je recommande !

Having to pay to sleep in your own car is never pleasant. Above all, you don't expect that after spending more than $40 for a night you will be woken up at 7 o'clock by someone using the blower for some reason, given that it is a windy and rainy day.
We spent more than $40 to sleep in our own car near a secondary gate with no security pin or any lock, left open all night. We did not feel safe at all. So much so that during the afternoon we noticed more than one person parking their van in the adjacent school parking lot and freely entering to take a shower or prepare dinner. This made us feel unsafe and a bit mocked.
Being close to the gate and the road we enjoyed the noisy night traffic!
The kitchen, small but nice, is located almost 500 meters from the place assigned to us.
The bathrooms could be cleaned better and at the right time. This morning they started cleaning the bathrooms at 9 o'clock, the time when those who are about to leave use them.

Not recommended place!

We stayed here for one night on a powered site. The campsites are flat, but not big. So you will be close to your neighbors. Our neighbors took their television with them and watched outside movies with volume 99 the whole evening. It wasn’t a nice atmosphere unfortunately.

The kitchen facilities were good, but the bathroom area was a bit dirty. It smelled really bad the whole day. Sorry, but we thought it was a bit too expensive for what you get.

預訂了一個晚上的power site
出入有code輸入管制
我們的營地在後面的新區域
廁所是獨立一間一間的
使用完要拖地以便下一個遊客使用
整體環境不錯蠻大的
晚上10點過後保持安靜
基本的設備都有
Stayed in my caravan for 3 nights, pleasant place to stay. Had an on suite site, which was nice for a change, clean and tidy.
I recommend the park specifically for those travelling with there fur babies. All the staff are very friendly and helpful

We stayed in one of the 2 bedroom chalets and were so happy with it. These bigger chalets were new(ish) and very spacious, clean and well equipped with cooking equipment (stove, microwave, fridge), a big TV in the living room and another tv in one of the bedrooms. Service was great from start to finish and the grounds itself were clean and tidy. Highly recommended!

Nice level van site in the new area. Had minimal interaction with staff. Can get a bit noisy at night from near by housing and hoons using the road in as a drift strip and location would put me off a long stay but was ok for a few nights.

100% spend the $68 dollars for an en-suite unpowered campsite.

You get a plot that’s is huge, well sheltered and comes with its own bathroom. The bathroom is modern, clean and well looked after. Stocked with toilet paper!! Further to this, it’s warm and nestled away in a corridor, so is ideal if it rains a little to hide away in.

The plot also comes with an outside tap, fake grass and privacy.

The public amenities, including laundry, a sink and toilets were good also from my limited use of them. They also have a communal hot plate BBQ and seating area if needed.

Incredible idea and perfect execution.
Heaven on earth this place of the world is...the park is wonderful ,the town is honestly something to go see for yourself...beaches on par with Hawaii....just beautiful
We booked one of the new cabins at the park, it was so beautiful and the layout was perfect. The park itself was really cute and such a great spot for family and kids.
Great park, spacious sites, good location
Absolutely adored our stay at this park. Great amenities, great service from reception and nothing to complain about. Wonderful experience and wonderful atmosphere! Would come back again 100%
Ensuite camp sites are great, the shower/toilets  units are very new and clean.

This is the worst camp site I have ever stayed at! After driving for seven and a half hours from Perth to Esperance, we were glad to have finally arrived. However, the staff at Pink Lake turned out to be anything but welcoming. From the moment we arrived, it felt like we were being treated as a problem rather than guests. The time was 10:10pm and it was our first night staying here. We were seated outside our tent and a car sped straight towards us. The car stopped about a metre from me with its high beams blinding us. An aggressive man got out of the car and began yelling. It felt like a scene from wolf creek. This was Tim and he made it clear that he didn’t want us here. He was rude and unreasonable and forced us to get to bed at once. He also threatened to pack up our belongings and kick us out if we didn’t do what he demanded. Tim stayed shining his high beams until we went to bed. I was brushing my teeth and finally saw him reversing away. I waved goodbye to him and then he sped straight for me, almost hitting me for the second time. He began yelling again, this time he seemed triggered by my wave and said we were being smart. Tim approached us the next day at 3pm. He threatened us again and said that he didn’t want a repeat of what happened last night. We certainly didn’t want that either and so we went to the camp office and explained how we felt. We spoke to Dianne who is the camp manager and she got her husband Tim to join the conversation. Tim said he was being proactive but it’s not okay for him to threaten people just because he thinks they might cause a problem. I understand the importance of having a quiet campsite however we were not being noisy and we weren’t going to be noisy. If you’re part of a young group looking for a pleasant place to camp, I suggest finding somewhere else. We won’t be returning.
Son muy cómodas las habitaciones y es un ambiente muy familiar
Great base to explore the Esperance area. Friendly staff lots of shady sites.

We stayed in one of the Thistle Cove cabins and I would not recommend it at all. It was underwhelming to say the least. It was clearly a trailer with some tin put around it for walls (I say that because the tow bar at the front is still there).

The inside of the space is tiny; beds in the kitchen, a table squashed into one corner, making it impossible to to maneouver 4 people around the combined kitchen/living/bedroom.
The second bedroom had barely enough space to get around it; it fit the double bed and that was it.

The lino floor was coming up and the floorboards were uneven and the whole cabin shook when anyone walked around.

The bathroom was equally as small. Barely enough room to turn around in. Early-growth mould in the corner above the shower.
2 toilet rolls for 4 people for 5 days, with a charge for extra if needed.

A few of the flyscreens were torn and there were holes in the walls. Not to mention the very out-of-date power points.

There must be nicer cabins as we were right next to one that looked much newer, but the Thistle Cove cabins are out-of-date & in need of updating ASAP.

The park itself is well maintained; lots of activities for kids, feels safe and other guests are quiet and respectful. Bathroom & laundry well-maintained. I would stay again at the park, but not if the Thistle Cove cabins were the only option.

TLDR: Bring a clothes horse. Dont ask for towels, they'll get mad.

* I hired a cabin for 1 week at the park 2025 new years.
The cabin was small, but that wasn't never the issue. The biggest issue was the the "bathroom" segment of the cabin, had no drain.
The shower has a curtain but it does not keep in all the water, and the little room floods. Without a drain, the bathmat has zero chance of absorbing the water.

* I went to the front desk after a 3 days and asked for more towels and there was no compassion. I got a response of "we only give you new towels after 1 week" and after explaining the situation I was in, the manager came out and looked at me like she was scolding me - like it was my fault I could be on top of keeping the room dry.

* I know when you goto Esperance, you have little options and this place might be your only hope and if you want a cabin, I recommend bringing alot of extra towels even if you have a long stay and definitely bring a towel horse, you will need it.

Oh - the pluses of the place, is their strict curfew on noise, reasonable parking, great air conditioner and its bedding is clean (compared to other accommodation in Esperance)

Do not waste your time and money and go somewhere else. Myself and a group of friends booked in to stay here and were instantly met with not only terrible customer service but honestly just a terrible human interaction.

On our first night a few of us were sitting around having a civilised chat when we were blinded by some high beams. Out pops Tim, all guns blazing. Apparently there had been a noise complaint coming from our general area which is hard to believe considering a) we were all pretty tired after driving the whole day to get there, and b) we had only returned from the camp kitchen a mere 5 minutes beforehand.

Clearly his tactic was inTIMidation as right off the bat he approached us with an unnecessary amount of aggression even though we were all completely complacent.
We all went to bed after that because he told us if we didn't we would personally pack all of our things for us. He left and after 5 minutes doubled back and had another go at two of my friends who were still up purely for the purpose of brushing their teeth. It is hard to believe that maintaining a quiet campsite was his motive as he caused a ruckus much loud than any noise we would have been making.

We got the vibe that because we were a young group of friends he was quick to jump to conclusions assuming we would be loud disrespectful. We left the next day even though we planned on staying there for 3 more nights because we all felt very unwelcome and uncomfortable.

We had a fantastic 2 bedroom unit which was fantastic, very clean. And spacious and a wonderful experience with a well organised management team that were so friendly and kept us up to date via text messages with details
Fantastic and we walked into town which was a lovely 25 minute walk. Close to IGA liquorland and bakery. Highly recommend
Thank you
Great place to stay, ideal location, camp kitchen could be bigger for this massive site, enjoyed our stay in the non-powered sites. Recommended.

Stayed at unpowered site here on a recent trip to Esperance and to be honest I wouldn’t come back. The sites are right on top of each-other and a decent walk to any facilities. If you can camp more self sustainably id go elsewhere

Great stay at the Pink Lake Esperance Caravan Park. Staff are friendly, its well maintained and clean. Kid friendly with play equipment. Laundry and ablutions block are also clean. We had a lot of fun here as we had a big open area for the boys to play and ride their bikes. I would definitely stay here again in the future and would also recommend this place to others that are considering a stay here.

We have just had a great stay here with a powered site. All the staff are very friendly and helpful. We just wish our fellow travellers would observe the polite message in the excellent shower blocks!! We would highly recommend Pink Lake. Thanks again. Cheryl and Roy. (UK)

My Partner and i stayed here for 4 nights at one of the campsites and it was great! there is plenty of room for your camp gear as well as your car and all the facilities were very clean. Staff are wonderful as well!

Good all round. The new established area up the back is fantastic.
Dog friendly.
Nice spot.
Plenty to see around Esperance and this is a good base for that.
Plenty of room for a 40 foot motorhome. Great staff

Really nice caravan park. The staff was very friendly and attentive. We stayed in the cabin N6 and it was great. Very comfy bed and pillows. The shower was hot and powerful and the kitchen well-equipped. Wifi works súper fast. I would recommend this lace to anyone wanting to stay a few nights in  Esperance.
Great park, very roomy. Ablutions very clean.
I was surprised at the value for money. Extremely tidy room and well kept property.
Beautiful park, very clean and staff really helpful. We stayed on a powered site which was very roomy and one of the best sites we have visited. We absolutely loved it 😊

Wir waren hier 3 Nächte mit unserem Van. Die Toiletten und Duschen sind sehr sauber! Die Küche hat alles, was man braucht und es gibt ein Bücherregal, wo man Bücher tauschen kann. ☺️ Das Personal ist super lieb und der Campingplatz hat eine gute Lage. (8 Minuten mit dem Auto zu Twilight Beach)
Very clean park and cabins.
Nice beautiful place to stay in, service great

This is the best caravan park we have stayed in for a very very long time. ( We have stayed in lots btw) The new section with ensuites were superb large lots, the 'turf' amazing, staff friendly and helpful, safe, secure, All
round AWESOME! The gardens were wonderful. Could recommend this place to any one as there is such a huge variety of accommodation.
We stayed on site with own amenities. Very clean spotless place lovely people.very homely feel
It was really nice. I stayed in the van on an ensuite site
The front of house staff were excellent. Very friendly.  Good sized sites. Amenities close by

We have stayed here for 6 nights & have very much enjoyed the friendly, helpful & happy service from Di & her team. The amenities are very clean & tidy as were the 2 card-operated laundries. We also enjoyed the green spaces, lovely flower & herb gardens, the clean & well organised camp kitchen & ‘family’ space - especially during the wet & windy weather!
Pleasantly surprised! Definitely worth the money. Lovely staff!

I was pleasantly surprised by these budget cabins. I only needed a bed to sleep for one night in Esperance before heading back to Perth the following day, so I booked one of those $100 budget cabins. When I arrived, I was pleasantly surprised by the size and cleanliness of these cabins. They come equipped with a microwave, fridge, sink, dining table and many beds. The only thing is, they don't have an ensuite bathroom, but it's a tourist park, so I didn't mind that. The toilet and shower were only 5 metres away from my cabin. I really enjoyed my stay there and for the price they offer incredible value. I can highly recommend these cabins to everyone looking for a budget option in Esperance.
Lovely place . Very clean.  Great location. Very small cabin, but cheap enough
Great facilities, very friendly staff. Really close to town.
Good 👍

We stayed in the park for 3 nights in a Cabin.
The cabin was in a pristine condition.
Di & Ian we fabulous. They made our stay.
We highly recommend this park.

Seriously 5 star place. We extended our stay from 4 nights to 6 after just one night. Stayed in one of the new chalets and it was beautiful. After a 7 hour drive the chalet was luxurious. The finishing details superb. The park itself is so tidy and the staff super friendly. The park is close to everything in Esperance. Will be recommending to everyone we know and definitely will be back.
Thank you for a wonderful stay. 👍
It's clean and ok, facilities very dated.
Great value for money, getting an end of day price.  Cabin had all anyone could need to be comfortable, although it was a bit squishy getting around the bed.
Rooms are small, floors squeek, taps drip all night

Amenities are old but clean. Only 2 showers in mens. It bothers me when fees are rated to an extra $00.50. Squeezing every cent they can. Wont let you in until you read terms and condition with signature. All care, no responsibility. Nice park but not worth the fee they charge.
Location a little out of town but nice and quiet and s lovely oark amenities clean the site we had was a good size for a 22 foot van
Has everything you need
Very clean and quiet. Good price. Thank you.
Very clean amenities, well maintained grounds, lovely well appointed pet friendly cabin. Will definately be back :)
Great location, all amenities in good order. Very busy when we went.

Great park, amenities very good.
Wanted a grassed site but it was super busy, only sites left were on a new gravel area. So glad we took the gravel site as there was so much more room, every site has a grass mat. It will look good when its finished.

Booked this campground specifically for their wifi to get some work done and some emails out. But when checking in was when they announced to wifi's been off for over a month, waiting for a technician to come and fix it. If you've been waiting for over a month for someone to stop by it's either time to find a new technician or just scratch the option of internet from your website.

Neat and tidy caravan park with all the basics. Kids playground not as new or modern as elsewhere  but entertained our kids well.
Unfortunately during our visit there was only 1 out of 3 dryers  working. It it appears they where onto of getting them removed/replaced.  Location was OK. The Local IGA is overpriced.
We stayed for 1 night over Easter and so it was very busy. Its popular  with families and is well maintained.

A great little park. Nice grassy sites with shadecloth/fake grass sections. Trees and well shaded sites available. Staff rearranged things when we wanted to stay another few nights without changing sites. Amenities are well set up and spacious.

Stayed a week ago,...would have stayed longer but booked out.
Nice park on grass site & facilities all good. Bad side was entrance to park on a bend & when  no room to park near entrance,...situation becomes dangerous.
Pink lake has been very accommodating we've moved between room and powered tent site and has been easy transition. Amenities are clean and there is a few kids love jumping pillow and playground
Les sanitaires, cuisine, salles de repas sont propres.

Booked a week ahead, was offered sleep 5 when I advised only 2. Got to the spot after 6 hours of travelling, unloaded bags, had a cuppa, chilled for about 2 hours and this damp, wet dog smell hit us, we opened door, windows. My stomach was turning. Spoke to the receptionist who managed to find us a cabin which would have suited my initial requirements. All of a sudden owners pitched up and said that I have a problem with the room. I said yes the carpets stink. Well according to the owners they just had it renovated a month ago. It's a family chalet so how many families stayed before we came. Who knows, it was stale and stank. I was going to take the alternative but due to their rudeness and arrogancy and them offering me my money back without me negotiating, we left.
I paid cash and they wanted to reimburse me on my card, I said no pay me the cash I paid and the deposit U took off my card.l put back on the card.  Not waiting 7 days. Money has still not reflected my account for deposit paid. I believe it takes 7 days.will check again. This is a holiday I have waited 17 years for. Step up and make your holiday makers feel welcome, deal with the situation professionally. I did not argue. I said the carpets 🦨 what else must I say. I was spending 5 days there. I would not have dealt with that smell for 5 days.
Friendly staff and we stayed in an unpowered site which had grass.  Amenities were clean but old.
The location was good great beaches to far too go for a couple of days but we had a great time fantastic
Staff were very nice and welcoming but the managers were unbelievable rude and unprofessional. Would definitely not recommend going there!

The staff were helpful and nice but the camps were squished and completely unorganised. Extension leads plugged in everywhere, random buildings cramped amongst van sites. We personally stayed at a site squished between two buildings and a fence. Wasn’t Homely
Got a powered caravan site, and it was great. It had plenty of shade and a concrete pad for the awning. The park managers were also very helpful, guiding us to our spot down the back.

Can’t get in before 12. Caravan pad that we had was small and needed extra levelling blocks. Super tight roadways and no clear parking areas. Staff pleasant and facilities clean. Trees in the front half, but up the back (for bigger vans and buses) there’s nothing. Big hot open area. No pool.

We recently stayed at Esperance Pink Lake Tourist Park for four nights and overall enjoyed the experience. The front part of the park had a nice atmosphere, and the camping kitchen was well-equipped. The amenities were clean and well-maintained. However, we encountered a frustrating incident when charging a small power bank for our phones. It disappeared, and after some inquiry, I discovered it at the reception. The receptionist told me that charging devices on an unpowered site is not allowed. Given the minimal cost of around 6 Cents and our $160 expenditure, this rule seems illogical. It's disappointing that such a minor issue could impact our overall impression, making us consider other options for our next visit.
Great park, great caravan sites, great management & staff, courteous, professional, and very, very helpful. Thanks to all who helped make our stays with you so easy and enjoyable!

$50 for a powered site on a dirt block, which is basically a building site, is expensive. I’m sure it’ll be good once the expansion is completed. The esperance bay holiday park is cheaper and much nicer and is walking distance to everything!
Very unfriendly and rude reception lady staff. I regret staying here and will never naver naver ever visit again.

Wouldn't hesitate to stay again. Pet friendly to.
Thanks for having us 😊

Don't waste your money and go elsewhere. We have been on the road for 8 months and this is by far the worst experience we've had in a caravan park. Managers were so rude, unwelcoming and unprofessional. You'll have staff patrolling your camps during the night. Two separate staff had come and accused us without uniforms on after 7pm. Really horrible people to deal with.
Given wifi password, however, you won't actually be able to access the WiFi anywhere.
We've also used public amenities nicer than the ones they offer here.
Après avoir fait plusieurs camping dans la région je peux dire que celui ci est vraiment bien il y a vraiment tout ce qu’il faut que ce soit pour la cuisine ou les salles de bains !

Old fashion service and accommodation/sites. No beating around the bush. What you see is what you get. Brought back memories from when we were young ( kids). There is nothing wrong if you don't expect an over the top camping site. Love it!
Great structure, clean toilets, big bbq area and friendly staff. We loved our stay!

HORRIBLE! The campsite is very expensive for what it is, and the pitches are bland and unkempt. The lady at reception is unbearable, she doesn't hesitate to take herself for a little boss in front of the customers by talking badly and putting them down without scruple. We were very disappointed, even though we had good preconceptions about Australians...

I don’t normally reply to reviews as everyone is entitled to their opinion but….. I am sorry you feel that way Guillamme but you forgot to mention that you were using our park as a halfway house well after checkout time. You were asked more than once to leave as checkout is at 10am, 2 1/2 hours later you are still helping yourself to our parks facilities. I am a very fair person but you and your 7 friends outstayed your welcome. Have a lovely day and enjoy the rest of your trip

Our first time here in the park and also in the city. We were lucky and got a site in the new part in the back. Large space, easy to reverse into. There is a new amenities building that has nice toilet and shower combo ensuites. They are clean and well maintained. Staff was friendly and very helpful over the phone and in person too. There is a huge jumping platform for kids.
Location is very much the worst thing, staff and rooms are good.
Great location. Nice and clean.

Very bad costumer service, the lady at the reception was very unfriendly and had no manners what so ever. Horrible toilets and showers no pressure from the shower heads and no hot water. Small kitchen can’t cook anything on a plate in the microwave. Camp kitchen is way too far for us to walk to cook and wash dishes and sink is tiny. Don’t even come to shut the gates and leave open all night. Toilets/bathrooms are tiny can’t even move around with more than 1 person in there, toilets are blocked and don’t flush properly.
Great place most definitely stay there again and great butcher 1 minute away, try there feta and olive snags (to die for )
Stayed here in our campervan for 2 nights. Good location, lovely check in woman. Clean toilets and showers. Would recommend

Ein mit Liebe geführter Park, das merkt man. Besonders schön fand ich den kräutergarten, wo man sich frische Kräuter nehmen darf. Sehr schöne Lage, nur 5 min zum twighlight beach. Hat alles super geklappt und war auch immer sauber.

Highly recommend this park, our travels from QLD to WA we’ve stayed at a number of parks and this would be right up in the top of our list of parks to recommend to friends and family. Had such a welcoming feel, staff, managers all so happy and welcoming. Facilities are top notch and a great location to head out to the beaches along the beautiful coast. Will be back one day !

grosszügige und saubere Duschen/WCs.
Gut ausgestatteter Bookchange.

Das Wifi ist an der Reception ok. auf unserem Platz Nr.29c war es nicht nur schlecht, es war schlichtweg nicht vorhanden, obwohl ich an der Reception gefragt habe, ob wir einen Platz mit gutem WiFi bekommen könnten, da ich ein paar Mails, Finanzen ect checken müsse. Als ich den Platz wechseln wollte, liessen sie es nicht zu und behaupteten, es wäre ein Verstärker in der Nähe unseres Stellplatzes. ich solle es da versuchen. das hat leider nicht geklappt. und dann war niemand mehr an der Reception... leider bin ich anständig erzogen, sonst hätte ich einfach den Platz gewechselt. es ärgert mich sehr, für diesen Platz $70 bezahlt zu haben. nur zum Übernachten hätte ein Rastplatz gereicht.
Stayed on powered site for a few days. Great facilities, people were pleasant and great friendly service. Not in the middle of town but still a great quiet location. Will come back again.

One of the best caravan park we've stayed at after 7 months on the road. The managers are ridiculously friendly, helpful and hardworking. this then flows down through all the staff we encountered and results in such a nice park.

The amenities are constantly spotless, camp kitchen, bbqs the same. Grass areas are lush and green with  a courtesy well cared for herb garden for guests to use. Nice quiet area and decent site size. Honestly can't fault the place. Cheers for a great stay guys.
Stayed in November, what a great park, well maintained, big showers, nice shady trees, would definitely recommend.

Referred to this park by another CV Park in town as they were full. About 3km out of town has everything you need. We'll run, clean amenities and IGA, bakery, butcher and takeaway just down the road. Would definately away again

Just finished a four night stay in one of the on site villas.
I've been on the road for almost twelve weeks and this was by far my favourite stopover.
Aimee and Trevor were both super friendly and super helpful...any question or request was answered in no time at all.
Other staff I interacted with were just as good.
My room was clean and comfortable...I slept as well as if I was at home in my own bed.
A great place to stay.
Great park with friendly staff. Really good camp kitchen and amenities.
Stayed there for 2 nights. Just went there without a booking, not a problem. Really friendly staff, clean toilets and big showers.

We unfortunately got the yappy dogs so this sucked, next door,  sausage dogs. If you have dogs in your  van , be respectful of others who dont.
The hot water was limited in showers but is a beautiful caravan park . Clean. Enjoyed our stay as esperence us beautiful.

We have never stayed here before but will definitely be back. It is very quiet, the ablutions are spotless, the staff are friendly. The park are in the process of extending the sites available with little inconvenience to visitors. Thank you
What fantastic hosts were Trevor and Amy.Always happy and smiling and up for a chat.Great powered site in a nice quiet area.Ensuite amenities always cleaned and maintained.

Great location
Friendly and helpful staff
Clean and tidy park
Great kids facilities

I had 3 nights here in an unpowered site. Lovely green grass, clean amenities and wonderful friendly staff. Neighbourhood dogs are an issue during the day but I never heard them after dark. A few small potholes within the park but I believe they will be fixed after the school holidays in Oct.

We loved our 1 night stay at Pink Lake Tourist Park in our RV.  Aimee was so friendly and helpful, and her local knowledge very much appreciated.
The park is lovely and clean, with very large, clean amenities.
The park was very busy, and yet it was lovely and quiet at night.  We had a great nights sleep.
We would definitely recommend.

I spent a month at the Esperance Pink Lake Tourist Park and couldn’t be happier with my stay. The staff were friendly and attentive. The facilities were clean and the room was cozy. I’d recommend a stay here!

Had an amazing stay in cabin no.4. Was greeted by Aimee who was friendly and most helpful. Cabin was excellent. Clean and well appointed. The park overall had a really nice feel to it. Could not fault this park in any way and one of the best we have ever visited in our Australia wide journey. Highly recommend and will definitely stay again when in Esperance.

Disgusting customer service with violent fueled anger run by hearsay and untrue rumours of made-up by the ladies who obviously needs education in mental health and anger management.  Do not recommend at all.

Nice and roomy .
Clean,easy to get to town .
Near the ocean drive tourist road.
Lovely town. Must check out a store called From Anything and Everything  Esperance.
What a fantastic place to stop for the night or a holiday great management.
Everything was perfect!

Nice park with grassy sites
Someone was trying to hack my phone when I hooked up to the free Wi fi

We recently stayed here for 10 nights as a family of 5 and absolutely loved it. They had a bouncing pillow and playground that the kids loved. Beds were really comfy, staff were excellent and very helpful. The microwave in our room wasn't working, an hour later they came and changed it for a new one for us.

No complaints, really good prices for what you get as well. Would definitely stay here again.
Managers very helpful and friendly.  Lovely park great  amenities,  close to town
The receptionist was fantastic. The villa was clean tidy and more than I thought. One of the best on our travels.

7 of us with caravans stayed at the Pink Lake cv/pk.The weather was bad but we enjoyed our stay . Staff were extremely friendly.The new managers Trev and Aimee are doing a fabulous job.The facilities  are clean and spacious.We will return,can't wait to see the new alterations  later in the year.

I left a review 7 months ago about my how awful our stay was.
FAST FORWARD to 2nd stay and am editing my review. Very impressed! I'd definitely recommend this place. New owners... no idea if that has anything to do with it but I'll take that. Lady at the front counter was lovely. Was a quick process. We loved our room, it was very clean and the shower was nice and hot. Esp for the price. Will be back when we come this way again.
Good size blocks new amenities block are excellent. Nice and quiet only 5 min to town.

Stayed on powered site. Site had artificial turf for annex. Service was excellent both when booking and staying. Amenities roomy and clean. Obvious improvements are ongoing. Highly recommend this park.
Friendly staff,  good amenities, good sites. Playground for the kids. Would recommend to all.
Nice Park
Great service and really nice cabin ,clean and staff heaps nice, big call out to dawn, she is lovely and looks after you.
Great location and easy to get into town.
